categories.title: Emotion Control
categories.description: LoopTTS generates speech with target emotional expression guided by prosodic instructions.

samples.instruction: [e.g., Speak with a happy tone at moderate speed and high pitch.]

categories.title: Global Prosody (Speed & Pitch)
categories.description: LoopTTS controls global prosodic attributes including speaking speed and pitch level.

samples.instruction: [e.g., Speak at a fast speed with high pitch.]

categories.title: Word-Level Stress
categories.description: LoopTTS places emphasis on specific words as directed by the refine instruction.

samples.instruction: [e.g., Stress the word 'never' and stress the word 'always'.]

categories.title: Pause Insertion
categories.description: LoopTTS inserts deliberate pauses at specified positions to improve prosodic phrasing.

samples.instruction: [e.g., Insert a pause after 'however' and a pause after 'conclusion'.]

categories.title: Refinement with Instruction
categories.description: LoopTTS refines a prosodically defective utterance using a structured refine instruction from the AudioLLM Judge, producing corrected speech while preserving speaker identity.

samples.text: I've told you 100 times—don't open the door at night.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Fearful emotion. Specifically, stress the word '100', insert a pause after 'times', stress the word 'don't', and stress the word 'night'.

samples.text: The letter slipped from his grasp, a paper cut of bitter news.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Sad emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'slipped', insert a pause after 'grasp', and stress the word 'bitter'.

samples.text: The car door slammed shut, punctuating the end of the argument.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Angry emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'slammed', stress the word 'shut', insert a pause after 'shut', and stress the word 'argument'.

samples.text: I don't just understand you, Anne,' he said hesitatingly. 'You appear to be vexed about something.
samples.instruction: Specifically, stress the word 'just', stress the word 'vexed', and insert a pause after 'Anne'.
